paper_title: Palaeoclimate and Tortonian Climate Model Study

methods.description: This method involves reconstructing past climate conditions using proxy-based climate data from European palaeofloras. The Coexistence Approach (CA) is used to determine the climatic ranges in which a maximum number of nearest living relatives (NLRs) of fossil flora can coexist, providing insights into the palaeoclimatic conditions.

steps.description: Proxy-based climate data are obtained from a total of 75 European palaeofloras comprising pollen and spores, leaf floras, and carpological data.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain proxy-based climate data from European palaeofloras

steps.description: All floras were analyzed using the Coexistence Approach (CA).
Calculate the Köppen Aridity Index (AI) using annual precipitation divided by mean annual temperature plus a constant.
Use the means of CA intervals in the calculation to visualize climatic trends between regions.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize climatic trends using CA interval means

steps.description: Infer the summer wind direction based on the overall distribution of WMP.
steps.searchable_summary: infer summer wind direction from WMP distribution

methods.description: This method uses a fully coupled global climate model (ECHAM5/MPI-OM) to simulate the Tortonian climate, demonstrating atmospheric circulation changes implied by WMP reconstruction. The model includes modifications to physical boundary conditions to better represent the Tortonian climate.

steps.description: Employ a Tortonian climate modelling study based on a fully coupled global climate model (ECHAM5/MPI-OM).
steps.searchable_summary: use ECHAM5/MPI-OM for Tortonian climate modeling

steps.description: Modify physical boundary conditions in the model to better represent the Tortonian climate.
steps.searchable_summary: modify model boundary conditions for Tortonian climate

steps.description: Set the CO2 concentration in the Tortonian simulation to 360 ppm.
steps.searchable_summary: set CO2 concentration to 360 ppm in simulation
